comment: Flow_Intake_SharedMailbox_<Mailbox> — the PARAMETERISED, per-inbox multi-mailbox intake (docs/plans/phase-2-live-activation/multi-inbox-access.md Option A). ONE definition ships to ANY inbox: the ONLY per-copy difference is the IntakeMailbox flow-parameter value bound to the V2 trigger's mailboxAddress. Logic is IDENTICAL to flows/definitions/intake.definition.json (Message-ID get-or-create dedup + MinIntakeDate go-live guard + concurrency=1 + best-effort provider-resolve + sourceMailbox provenance) — it mirrors collisioncc graph-intake.ts ingestGraphMessage, never calls it. Trigger is SharedMailboxOnNewEmailV2 (operationId; param mailboxAddress, reconciled live 2026-06-18 against the Office 365 connector swagger). SUPPORTS BOTH credential scenarios with the SAME definition because only the bound CONNECTION differs, never the JSON: (a) SHARED MAILBOX that the connected digital@ holds Exchange Full Access to -> REUSE the existing cr1bd_sharedmailbox_office365 connection (digital@, id bd752b83172a4e99b3db595942f1b30f); NO new password / NO new connection. (b) LICENSED USER MAILBOX with its own identity -> bind cr1bd_sharedmailbox_office365 to a NEW Office 365 Outlook connection created by an interactive sign-in AS that user (real OAuth/MFA), OR (preferred) grant digital@ Full Access and treat it exactly like (a). The connectionName stays shared_office365 in BOTH cases (the only Office 365 ref in connection-references.json; the connector swagger exposes SharedMailboxOnNewEmailV2 there). Per-inbox how-to + the Get-Mailbox/Get-MailboxPermission Full-Access checks live in docs/activation/multi-inbox-activation.md. [BUILD] only; pointing any trigger at a live Collision Engineers mailbox + all live-inbox tests are [RESERVED-FOR-USER]. IntakeMailbox is ALWAYS a flow parameter, NEVER a hardcoded live address. GO-LIVE HARDENING (identical to intake.definition.json): (1) MinIntakeDate guard (String param, default '2026-06-17', set per-inbox at activation = that inbox's go-live date) — FIRST action after the trigger drops any email whose receivedDateTime is before MinIntakeDate (Terminate Succeeded) so a freshly-connected inbox never ingests its historical backlog. (2) hasAttachments=true is a TEMPORARY no-attachment-noise filter at the trigger source, to be REMOVED once the later email-management / routing system lands. CROSS-INBOX SAFETY: the Internet Message-ID is unique per email regardless of which inbox saw it, so the Find_existing_by_messageId get-or-create guard de-dups even if the SAME email lands in two inboxes (To: inbox-2, Cc: inbox-3) — worst case a benign duplicate_dropped audit on the second arrival. Each flow keeps its OWN concurrency=1 (serialises within one mailbox); the N flows run independently in parallel, which is correct because the dedup keys are global.

metadata.description: The ONE per-inbox knob. Shared/licensed inbox address bound to the V2 trigger's mailboxAddress AND stamped on every Case as cr1bd_sourcemailbox for per-inbox attribution. Flow parameter so ONE definition ships to ALL inboxes. NEVER a hardcoded live mailbox in the authored definition (linter check #4). Set to the watched address per copy at activation: e.g. one flow IntakeMailbox=digital@…, a second IntakeMailbox=<inbox-2>, a third IntakeMailbox=<inbox-3>.

Inclusive earliest email receivedDateTime to ingest (ISO date/datetime). Set PER-INBOX at activation = that inbox's go-live date. Drop_if_before_min_date drops any email received BEFORE this instant (Terminate Succeeded) so a newly-connected inbox — which can hold years of mail — never ingests its historical backlog. Default 2026-06-17.

parameters.item/cr1bd_after: @concat('mailbox=', parameters('IntakeMailbox'), '; receivedDateTime=', coalesce(triggerOutputs()?['body/receivedDateTime'], ''), ' < MinIntakeDate=', parameters('MinIntakeDate'), ' -> dropped (go-live min-date guard)')

Drop_if_before_min_date.comment: Go-live min-date guard (identical to intake.definition.json, with mailbox= added to the audit for per-inbox attribution). FIRST action after the trigger: if the email's receivedDateTime is BEFORE MinIntakeDate (default 2026-06-17), audit a dropped_before_min_date event (reuses the duplicate_dropped action value 100000005 — no schema change) then Terminate Succeeded. Otherwise the else-branch is empty and the dedup/ingest chain proceeds. Robust comparison via ticks() so format/timezone differences do not skew the test.

Init_payloadHash.comment: payloadHash seed = normalised subject + from; the attachment-loop flow (classify-persist, §5.2) folds in sorted attachment SHA256s before the second dedup probe. SHA256 is computed in the storage Function, not in Power Fx.

Init_senderDomain.comment: Sender email domain (after @), lowercased — resolves the WorkProvider by knownEmailDomains (ADR-0011 domain-only match). 'none.invalid' sentinel when absent keeps the $filter valid.

Find_existing_by_messageId.comment: Exact Message-ID probe = the get-or-create idempotency guard (alternate key cr1bd_case_sourcemessageid_key). GLOBAL across inboxes: the Internet Message-ID is unique per email regardless of which mailbox saw it, so this also prevents a double-create when the SAME email lands in two intake inboxes. Combined with this flow's trigger concurrency=1, prevents double-create on Graph retries too.

parameters.item/cr1bd_after: @concat('mailbox=', parameters('IntakeMailbox'), '; messageId=', variables('messageId'), ' already ingested -> dropped (ADR-0010 exact repeat)')

Resolve_provider.comment: Resolve the WorkProvider by sender email domain (ADR-0011 domain-only match). Best-effort: If_one_provider runs after Succeeded OR Failed so a filter quirk or zero match never sinks the Case create. NOTE: this mirrors intake.definition.json's CURRENT Resolve_provider exactly (provider-match exact-membership hardening is tracked separately in intake.definition.json + provider-match.definition.json; this multi-inbox copy deliberately stays in lockstep with the live intake logic and is NOT the place to diverge).

Create_case_matched.comment: Exactly one provider matched the sender domain -> create the Case (new_email) bound to it. cr1bd_sourcemailbox = the per-flow IntakeMailbox gives correct per-inbox attribution for free.

Create_case_unassigned.comment: No single provider match (0 or ambiguous, or resolve unavailable) -> create the Case (new_email) UNASSIGNED; staff assign the provider in review. Never fails on an empty lookup. cr1bd_sourcemailbox still records the watched inbox.

If_one_provider.comment: Graceful provider gating: bind only on an unambiguous single match; otherwise create unassigned. runAfter Succeeded+Failed so an unmatched/unavailable resolve still creates the Case.

If_already_ingested.comment: Message-ID hit = exact-repeat half of ADR-0010 -> drop. The payloadHash probe (same shape, $filter on cr1bd_payloadhash) runs in the classify-persist flow once attachment hashes are folded in.
